title: TrustForge Agent Contract
description: Declarative contract that makes a TrustForge-enabled codebase legible and safe for AI agents. See TF-0006.

contract_version.description: Version of the agent-contract schema itself.

spec_version.description: TrustForge spec revision this contract conforms to.

project.description: Project identifier used in logs and contract references.

trust_domain.description: The TrustForge trust domain this project belongs to.

threat_model.description: Path to the project's threat-model manifest.

policy_engine.description: Policy backend in use by this project.

path.description: Path to the policy file when applicable.

actions_library.description: Standard actions library identifier, e.g. tf-actions-std@1.

target_sets.description: Named glob lists, reusable in action rules.

actions.description: Declared actions this project allows agents to perform.

integrations.description: Connections to MCP tools, ProofRPC services, and test commands.

items.description: Opaque MCP tool descriptor; shape defined by the MCP bridge spec.
Opaque ProofRPC service descriptor; shape defined by TF-0007.

test_commands.description: Shell commands that exercise the project's tests.

allow_targets.description: Glob patterns or @target-set references the action may target.

deny_targets.description: Glob patterns or @target-set references the action must not target.

allow_actors.description: Glob patterns matching caller actor URIs that MAY invoke this action. Empty / omitted means 'every authenticated actor is allowed' (subject to deny_actors and other guard rules). The matcher checks both the cryptographic actor URI (tf:actor:process:key/<thumbprint>) and the self-claimed peer_hint URI when present.

deny_actors.description: Glob patterns matching caller actor URIs that MUST NOT invoke this action. Overrides allow_actors. The matcher checks both the cryptographic actor URI and the self-claimed peer_hint URI.

parameters.description: Inline JSON Schema describing the parameters this action accepts.

reversible.description: Hint that this action can be inverted by its counterpart.

danger_tags.description: Structured danger categories; AI agents MUST escalate on destructive / irreversible / financial / security-sensitive tags.

pre_conditions.description: Named gates that must hold before this action may run.

reversal_note.description: If reversible is false, a note on how the action can be undone out-of-band (e.g. via VCS).
